column1: 8 ways to say you feel sad
Health Idioms | 1️⃣tough pill to swallow: difficult or unpleasant situation of fact to accept; Losing my job was a tough pill to swallow
column2: but I'm ready for new opportunities now. | 2️⃣fit as a fiddle: to be in very good health; Even at 80
column3: my grandfather is as fit as a fiddle. | 3️⃣under the weather: feeling ill or unwell; I'm feeling a bit under the weather today
column4: so I might stay in bed. | 4️⃣bite the bullet: to face a difficult or unpleasant situation with courage; I know the surgery is scary
column5: but you'll have to bite the bullet. | 5️⃣kick the bucket: a humorous or informal way to say someone has died; I'd like to see China before I kick the bucket.
